# `hcp-sbom` provisioner

The `hcp-sbom` provisioner uploads software bill of materials (SBOM) files from artifacts built by Packer to HCP Packer. You must format SBOM files you want to upload as JSON and follow either the [SPDX](https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/latest) or [CycloneDX](https://cyclonedx.org/) specification. HCP Packer ties these SBOM files to the version of the artifact that Packer builds.

The provisioner supports two modes of operation:

1. **Manual SBOM Upload**: Upload a pre-generated SBOM file from the remote host
2. **Automatic SBOM Generation**: Automatically generate an SBOM by downloading and running an SBOM generation tool on the remote host. By default, Packer uses [Syft](https://github.com/anchore/syft) for SBOM generation, but you can specify a custom tool if needed.

## Basic Example

The following example uploads an SBOM from the local `/tmp` directory and stores a copy at `./sbom/sbom_cyclonedx.json` on the local machine.

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCL2">

```hcl
provisioner "hcp-sbom" {
  source      = "/tmp/sbom_cyclonedx.json"
  destination = "./sbom/sbom_cyclonedx.json"
  sbom_name   = "sbom-cyclonedx"
}
```

</Tab>
<Tab heading="JSON">

```json
{
  "type": "hcp-sbom",
  "source": "/tmp/sbom_cyclonedx.json",
  "destination": "./sbom/sbom_cyclonedx.json",
  "sbom_name": "sbom-cyclonedx"
}
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Automatic SBOM Generation

The provisioner can automatically generate SBOMs by downloading and running an SBOM generation tool on the remote host. By default, Packer downloads and uses [Syft](https://github.com/anchore/syft) for SBOM generation. This eliminates the need for manual tool installation.

### Basic Auto-Generation Example

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCL2">

```hcl
provisioner "hcp-sbom" {
  auto_generate = true
  destination   = "./sbom"
  sbom_name     = "auto-generated-sbom"
}
```

</Tab>
<Tab heading="JSON">

```json
{
  "type": "hcp-sbom",
  "auto_generate": true,
  "destination": "./sbom",
  "sbom_name": "auto-generated-sbom"
}
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>

When `auto_generate` is enabled:
- Packer automatically detects the remote host's OS and architecture
- Downloads the appropriate Syft binary for SBOM generation (supports Linux, macOS, Windows on amd64/arm64)
- Uploads the SBOM generation tool to the remote host
- Executes the tool to generate an SBOM
- Downloads the SBOM and uploads it to HCP Packer
- Cleans up temporary files

### Custom SBOM Generation Tool Example

You can specify a custom SBOM generation tool and configuration instead of using the default Syft:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCL2">

```hcl
provisioner "hcp-sbom" {
  auto_generate    = true
  scanner_url      = "https://example.com/my-sbom-tool-linux-amd64"  # Must be direct binary, not archive
  scanner_checksum = "abc123..."
  scanner_args     = ["-o", "spdx-json", "--verbose"]
  scan_path        = "/opt/myapp"
  destination      = "./sbom"
  sbom_name        = "custom-tool-sbom"
}
```

</Tab>
<Tab heading="JSON">

```json
{
  "type": "hcp-sbom",
  "auto_generate": true,
  "scanner_url": "https://example.com/my-sbom-tool-linux-amd64",
  "scanner_checksum": "abc123def456789...",
  "scanner_args": ["-o", "spdx-json", "--verbose"],
  "scan_path": "/opt/myapp",
  "destination": "./sbom",
  "sbom_name": "custom-tool-sbom"
}
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>

### Elevated Execution Examples

#### Unix/Linux with Sudo

By default, the provisioner uses `sudo` for elevated permissions on Unix-like systems:

```hcl
provisioner "hcp-sbom" {
  auto_generate = true
  destination   = "./sbom"
  sbom_name     = "sbom-with-sudo"
  # Default execute_command includes sudo:
  # "chmod +x {{.Path}} && sudo {{.Path}} {{.Args}} {{.ScanPath}} > {{.Output}}"
}
```

For password-based sudo:

```hcl
provisioner "hcp-sbom" {
  auto_generate   = true
  execute_command = "chmod +x {{.Path}} && echo 'mypassword' | sudo -S {{.Path}} {{.Args}} {{.ScanPath}} > {{.Output}}"
  destination     = "./sbom"
  sbom_name       = "sbom-sudo-password"
}
```

Without sudo (if not needed):

```hcl
provisioner "hcp-sbom" {
  auto_generate   = true
  execute_command = "chmod +x {{.Path}} && {{.Path}} {{.Args}} {{.ScanPath}} > {{.Output}}"
  destination     = "./sbom"
  sbom_name       = "sbom-no-sudo"
}
```

#### Windows with Elevated User

For Windows hosts requiring administrative privileges:

```hcl
provisioner "hcp-sbom" {
  auto_generate      = true
  elevated_user      = "Administrator"
  elevated_password  = "MySecurePassword123!"
  destination        = "./sbom"
  sbom_name          = "windows-elevated-sbom"
}
```

## Configuration reference

You can specify the following configuration options.

Required parameters:

@include 'provisioner/hcp-sbom/Config-required.mdx'

Optional parameters:

@include '/provisioner/hcp-sbom/Config-not-required.mdx'

## OS and Architecture Detection

When `auto_generate` is enabled, the provisioner automatically detects the remote host's operating system and architecture by running:

- **SSH communicators**: `uname -s -m`
- **WinRM communicators**: `echo %PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE%`

This information is used to:
1. Download the correct Syft binary (or custom tool) for the target platform
2. Determine the appropriate archive format (.tar.gz for Unix/Linux/macOS, .zip for Windows)
3. Set platform-specific default execution commands

## Scanner Download and Archive Handling

### Default Behavior (Syft Auto-Download)

When `auto_generate` is enabled without specifying `scanner_url`, Packer automatically downloads Syft:

- **Unix/Linux/macOS**: Downloads `.tar.gz` archive, extracts locally, uploads binary to remote
- **Windows**: Downloads `.zip` archive, uploads zip to remote, extracts remotely (optimization for slow WinRM uploads)

> **Note**: Windows WinRM file uploads can be significantly slower than SSH due to protocol overhead. For better performance on Windows hosts, consider using SSH instead of WinRM as your communicator when possible. The provisioner includes optimizations (zip upload + remote extraction) to mitigate WinRM's slow upload speeds, but SSH will still provide better performance.

### Custom Scanner URL

When using `scanner_url` with a custom SBOM generation tool:

- **Direct binary URL**: The URL must point to a standalone binary file (not an archive)
- **Platform compatibility**: Ensure the binary is compatible with your target OS and architecture
- **No archive extraction**: Custom scanner URLs do not support archive formats - provide direct binary URLs only

Example of correct custom scanner URL:
```hcl
scanner_url = "https://example.com/my-scanner-linux-amd64"  # Direct binary
```

Example of incorrect custom scanner URL:
```hcl
scanner_url = "https://example.com/my-scanner.tar.gz"  # Archives not supported for custom URLs
```

## Complete Example with Manual Scanner Installation

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCL2">

```hcl
packer {
  required_plugins {
    docker = {
      version = ">= 1.0.0"
      source  = "github.com/hashicorp/docker"
    }
  }
}

source "docker" "ubuntu" {
  image  = "ubuntu:20.04"
  commit = true
}

build {
  sources = ["source.docker.ubuntu"]

  hcp_packer_registry {
    bucket_name = "test-bucket"
  }

  provisioner "shell" {
    inline = [
      "apt-get update -y",
      "apt-get install -y curl gpg",
      "bash -c \"$(curl -sSL https://install.mondoo.com/sh)\"",
      "cnquery sbom --output cyclonedx-json --output-target /tmp/sbom_cyclonedx.json",
    ]
  }

  provisioner "hcp-sbom" {
    source      = "/tmp/sbom_cyclonedx.json"
    destination = "./sbom"
    sbom_name   = "sbom-cyclonedx"
  }
}
```

</Tab>
<Tab heading="JSON">

```json
{
  "builders": [
    {
      "type": "docker",
      "image": "ubuntu:20.04",
      "commit": true
    }
  ],
  "provisioners": [
    {
      "type": "shell",
      "inline": [
        "apt-get update -y",
        "apt-get install -y curl",
        "bash -c \"$(curl -sSL https://install.mondoo.com/sh)\"",
        "cnquery sbom --output cyclonedx-json --output-target /tmp/sbom_cyclonedx.json"
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "hcp-sbom",
      "source": "/tmp/sbom_cyclonedx.json",
      "destination": "./sbom",
      "sbom_name": "sbom-cyclonedx"
    }
  ]
}
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Complete Example with Auto-Generation

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HCL2">

```hcl
packer {
  required_plugins {
    docker = {
      version = ">= 1.0.0"
      source  = "github.com/hashicorp/docker"
    }
  }
}

source "docker" "ubuntu" {
  image  = "ubuntu:20.04"
  commit = true
}

build {
  sources = ["source.docker.ubuntu"]

  hcp_packer_registry {
    bucket_name = "test-bucket"
  }

  # No need to manually install scanner tools!
  provisioner "hcp-sbom" {
    auto_generate = true
    scan_path     = "/"
    destination   = "./sbom"
    sbom_name     = "auto-generated-sbom"
  }
}
```

</Tab>
<Tab heading="JSON">

```json
{
  "builders": [
    {
      "type": "docker",
      "image": "ubuntu:20.04",
      "commit": true
    }
  ],
  "provisioners": [
    {
      "type": "hcp-sbom",
      "auto_generate": true,
      "scan_path": "/",
      "destination": "./sbom",
      "sbom_name": "auto-generated-sbom"
    }
  ]
}
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Template Variables in execute_command

The `execute_command` configuration option supports the following template variables:

- `{{.Path}}` - Path to the SBOM generation tool binary on the remote host
- `{{.Args}}` - Tool arguments (from `scanner_args`)
- `{{.ScanPath}}` - Path to scan (from `scan_path`)
- `{{.Output}}` - Output file path for the SBOM

These variables allow you to customize how the SBOM generation tool is executed while maintaining flexibility across different environments and security requirements.
